job_name: Pytorch FX Layer Tests / PyTorch FX Layer Tests
summary: Pytorch FX Layer Tests failed during dependency installation on Windows while pip attempted to resolve defusedxml==0.7.1 from PyPI.

root_cause.explanation: pip could not fetch or parse the simple index page for defusedxml on the Windows runner, so dependency resolution aborted before tests started. The error surfaced in the requirements_pytorch install step and is unrelated to the PR code changes.
evidence: 2026-06-11T18:16:19.7065545Z WARNING: Skipping page https://pypi.org/simple/defusedxml/ because the GET request got Content-Type: Unknown
2026-06-11T18:16:19.8141490Z ERROR: Could not find a version that satisfies the requirement defusedxml==0.7.1 (from versions: none)
2026-06-11T18:16:19.8158821Z ERROR: No matching distribution found for defusedxml==0.7.1
root_cause.failed_job: Pytorch FX Layer Tests / PyTorch FX Layer Tests

reproduction: On a Windows Python 3.11 environment, run `python -m pip install -r tests/requirements_pytorch` and observe failure when PyPI simple page for defusedxml is unreachable or returned with an unsupported Content-Type.
recommended_actions: Retry the workflow or rerun the dependency install step to confirm this is a transient PyPI/network issue.
If the failure repeats, inspect runner connectivity/proxy behavior for `https://pypi.org/simple/defusedxml/`.
Consider pinning a working wheel cache or mirror for this test job if the environment is known to be flaky.
prevention: Keep the PyTorch test dependency install isolated and retriable, and monitor for repeated PyPI access errors on Windows runners.
